audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for critique of scribes/Pharisees, seat of Moses, status titles, servant greatness, seven woes, justice/mercy/trust, inner/outer purity, prophets' blood, and Jerusalem lament.

verses.restored: Then Jesus spoke to the crowds and to his disciples,
saying, The scribes and the Pharisees sit on Moses' seat.
Therefore whatever they tell you, do and keep; but do not do according to their works, for they say and do not do.
They bind heavy burdens and lay them on people's shoulders, but they themselves are unwilling to move them with their finger.
All their works they do to be seen by people; they broaden their phylacteries and lengthen their fringes.
They love the first place at feasts and the first seats in the synagogues,
and greetings in the marketplaces and to be called Rabbi by people.
But you shall not be called Rabbi, for one is your Teacher, and you are all brothers and sisters.
Call no one your father on earth, for one is your Father, the heavenly one.
Nor be called leaders, because your Leader is one, the Messiah.
The greatest among you shall be your servant.
Whoever exalts self will be humbled, and whoever humbles self will be exalted.
Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, because you shut the reign of the heavens before people; for you do not enter, nor do you allow those entering to enter.
[Some later manuscripts add a woe concerning devouring widows' houses and long prayers.]
Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, because you cross sea and dry land to make one convert, and when he becomes one, you make him a son of Gehenna twice as much as yourselves.
Woe to you, blind guides, who say, Whoever swears by the temple, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gold of the temple is bound.
Fools and blind ones, which is greater: the gold, or the temple that makes the gold holy?
And, Whoever swears by the altar, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gift on it is bound.
Blind ones, which is greater: the gift, or the altar that makes the gift holy?
Therefore the one swearing by the altar swears by it and by all things on it.
The one swearing by the temple swears by it and by the One dwelling in it.
The one swearing by heaven swears by the throne of the Divine and by the One sitting on it.

Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, because you tithe mint, dill, and cumin, but have left the weightier things of the Law: justice, mercy, and trust. These it was necessary to do, without leaving the others.
Blind guides, straining out the gnat but swallowing the camel.
Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, because you cleanse the outside of cup and dish, but inside they are full of greed and self-indulgence.
Blind Pharisee, first cleanse the inside of the cup, so that the outside also may become clean.
Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, because you are like whitewashed tombs, which outwardly appear beautiful, but inside are full of dead bones and all uncleanness.
So you also outwardly appear righteous to people, but inside you are full of hypocrisy and lawlessness.
Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, because you build the tombs of the prophets and adorn the monuments of the righteous,
and say, If we had lived in the days of our fathers, we would not have shared with them in the blood of the prophets.
Thus you witness against yourselves that you are sons of those who murdered the prophets.
And you, fill up the measure of your fathers.
Serpents, offspring of vipers, how will you escape the judgment of Gehenna?
Because of this, look, I send to you prophets, wise ones, and scribes; some of them you will kill and crucify, and some you will flog in your synagogues and pursue from city to city,

so that upon you may come all righteous blood poured out on the earth, from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zechariah son of Barachiah, whom you killed between the sanctuary and the altar.
Amen, I say to you: all these things will come upon this generation.
Jerusalem, Jerusalem, killing the prophets and stoning those sent to her, how often I wished to gather your children as a hen gathers her chicks under the wings, and you were not willing.
Look, your house is left to you desolate.
For I say to you: you will certainly not see me from now until you say, Blessed is the one coming in the name of the Lord.
